title: paraspalathus ascendens
side_effects: Paraspalathus ascendens can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's extracts may also cause respiratory problems, such as coughing and shortness of breath. Improper handling of the plant can result in skin burns or rashes, requiring medical attention in severe cases.
